next_steps: Order ANCA, ANA, ENA, C3/C4 complement levels, and syphilis serology
Perform a punch biopsy of the rash for histopathology and direct immunofluorescence
Perform a nasal mucosal biopsy for vasculitis and granuloma formation
Obtain a computed tomography scan of the sinuses and high-resolution CT of the chest
Urinalysis with microscopic examination to assess for active renal involvement
Perform a bone marrow biopsy and aspirate for cytogenetics and UBA1 mutation testing if initial rheumatologic workup is unrevealing
